message:
feat(widgets): expand file picker tests; refine glyph atlas and WASM bridge

crates/ftui-widgets/src/file_picker.rs (+281 lines):
  Add comprehensive test coverage for the file picker widget: DirEntry
  edge cases (equality, clone, debug), FilePickerState navigation
  (enter/leave directory, toggle hidden, selection), and sorting
  behavior tests.

crates/frankenterm-web/src/glyph_atlas.rs:
  Refine atlas layout calculations and eviction logic.

crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs:
  Improve WASM bridge event handling and render loop coordination.

crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/test_logging.rs:
  Minor refinements to structured test output.

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2ky9","title":"E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# E2E Test Scripts with Comprehensive Logging

## Background
Plan Section Testing + Verification requires end-to-end tests that validate the complete pipeline from user input to terminal output. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging."

## E2E Test Philosophy
E2E tests should:
1. Test the complete system as a user would experience it
2. Run in a PTY to capture real terminal behavior
3. Log every step for post-mortem debugging
4. Be deterministic and reproducible
5. Cover happy paths and error cases

## E2E Test Script Structure

### Directory Layout
```
tests/
  e2e/
    fixtures/          # Test input files
    expected/          # Expected output snapshots
    scripts/           # Test runner scripts
      run_all.sh       # Master test runner
      test_inline.sh   # Inline mode tests
      test_altscreen.sh # Alt-screen tests
      test_cleanup.sh  # Cleanup/panic tests
      test_input.sh    # Input handling tests
    lib/
      common.sh        # Shared utilities
      logging.sh       # Logging functions
      pty.sh           # PTY spawning helpers
```

### Logging Framework (logging.sh)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive logging for E2E tests

LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-DEBUG}
LOG_FILE=${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/ftui_e2e_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log}

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    local caller="${BASH_SOURCE[2]}:${BASH_LINENO[1]}"
    echo "[$timestamp] [$level] [$caller] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_debug() { [[ "$LOG_LEVEL" == "DEBUG" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$@"; }
log_info()  { log "INFO" "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN" "$@"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "$@"; }

log_test_start() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "STARTING TEST: $test_name"
    log_info "=========================================="
}

log_test_pass() {
    local test_name=$1
    log_info "✓ PASSED: $test_name"
}

log_test_fail() {
    local test_name=$1
    local reason=$2
    log_error "✗ FAILED: $test_name"
    log_error "  Reason: $reason"
    log_error "  Log file: $LOG_FILE"
}

log_hex_dump() {
    local label=$1
    local data=$2
    log_debug "$label (hex): $(echo -n "$data" | xxd -p | tr -d n)"
}
```

## E2E Test Categories

### 1. Inline Mode Tests (test_inline.sh)
Tests that streaming logs + UI chrome works correctly.

```bash
test_inline_log_scroll() {
    log_test_start "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    
    # Setup
    local pty_output=$(mktemp)
    log_debug "PTY output file: $pty_output"
    
    # Run ftui app that streams 100 log lines with 6-line UI
    log_info "Spawning ftui inline mode app..."
    timeout 10s script -q -c "./target/debug/ftui-harness --mode inline --ui-height 6 --log-count 100" "$pty_output"
    local exit_code=$?
    log_debug "Exit code: $exit_code"
    
    # Verify
    log_info "Verifying output..."
    
    # Check scrollback preservation
    if grep -q "Log line 1" "$pty_output"; then
        log_debug "Early log lines found in scrollback ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "Early log lines missing from scrollback"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check UI region intact
    if tail -6 "$pty_output" | grep -q "\[Status\]"; then
        log_debug "UI region present at bottom ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Inline log scroll" "UI region not at expected position"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_test_pass "Inline mode: log scrolling"
    rm "$pty_output"
}
```

### 2. Cleanup Tests (test_cleanup.sh)
Tests that terminal is restored after exit/panic.

```bash
test_panic_cleanup() {
    log_test_start "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
    
    # Capture terminal state before
    local before_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state before: $before_state"
    
    # Run app that panics
    log_info "Running app that will panic..."
    ./target/debug/ftui-panic-test 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Capture terminal state after
    local after_state=$(stty -g)
    log_debug "Terminal state after: $after_state"
    
    # Verify restoration
    if [[ "$before_state" == "$after_state" ]]; then
        log_debug "Terminal state restored ✓"
    else
        log_test_fail "Panic cleanup" "Terminal state not restored"
        log_error "  Before: $before_state"
        log_error "  After:  $after_state"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cursor visible (SGR sequence check)
    log_info "Checking cursor visibility..."
    # ... additional checks ...
    
    log_test_pass "Panic cleanup: terminal restored"
}
```

### 3. Input Tests (test_input.sh)
Tests for keyboard, mouse, paste handling.

### 4. ANSI Output Tests
Verify presenter output matches expected sequences.

## Test Output Format

Each test run produces:
1. Console output (PASS/FAIL per test)
2. Detailed log file with timestamps
3. JSON summary for CI
4. PTY captures for failed tests

### JSON Summary Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-31T23:45:00Z",
  "total": 50,
  "passed": 48,
  "failed": 2,
  "skipped": 0,
  "duration_ms": 12345,
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_inline_log_scroll",
      "status": "passed",
      "duration_ms": 234,
      "log_lines": 45
    },
    {
      "name": "test_panic_cleanup",
      "status": "failed",
      "duration_ms": 567,
      "error": "Terminal state not restored",
      "log_file": "/tmp/ftui_e2e_20260131_234500.log"
    }
  ]
}
```

## E2E Test Inventory

| Test | Description | Priority |
|------|-------------|----------|
| inline_basic | Basic inline mode display | P0 |
| inline_log_scroll | Log scrolling preserves scrollback | P0 |
| inline_resize | Resize re-anchors UI | P0 |
| inline_cursor | Cursor position contract | P0 |
| altscreen_enter_exit | Alt-screen lifecycle | P0 |
| cleanup_normal | Terminal restored on exit | P0 |
| cleanup_panic | Terminal restored on panic | P0 |
| cleanup_io_error | Terminal restored on IO error | P1 |
| input_key_basic | Basic key parsing | P0 |
| input_key_modifiers | Modifier key detection | P1 |
| input_mouse_click | Mouse click handling | P1 |
| input_mouse_scroll | Mouse scroll handling | P1 |
| input_paste | Bracketed paste handling | P1 |
| input_focus | Focus events | P2 |
| ansi_sgr | SGR sequence output | P0 |
| ansi_osc8 | OSC 8 hyperlink output | P1 |
| ansi_sync | Synchronized output | P1 |
| unicode_basic | ASCII width handling | P0 |
| unicode_wide | CJK wide chars | P0 |
| unicode_emoji | Emoji rendering | P0 |
| unicode_zwj | ZWJ sequences | P1 |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] run_all.sh executes all E2E tests
- [ ] Each test has detailed logging
- [ ] Failed tests produce actionable diagnostics
- [ ] JSON summary generated for CI
- [ ] PTY captures preserved on failure
- [ ] Tests run in < 60 seconds total
- [ ] All P0 tests must pass for v1

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-2kj (Agent Harness exists to test)
- Requires: bd-10i.11.2 (PTY framework)
- Blocks: bd-2gx (Quality Gates)

## CI Integration
- Run on every PR
- Block merge on P0 test failures
- Upload log artifacts on failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:22:54.577032739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441537371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:49:32.441432504Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E scripts + harness env hooks; full run passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","depends_on_id":"bd-2kj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"bd-2ky9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E suite: 31 tests, 6 suites, all passing in 39s.  {"id":"bd-2lus","title":"Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner","description":"## Background
A macro recorder turns the demo into a reproducible, scripted showcase. It should record input events, play them back deterministically, and support curated scenarios.

## Scope / Goals
- Record input events with timestamps
- Play back with normalized timing
- Scenario runner for guided demos
- UI to view timeline and replay markers

## Tests / Logging
- Unit tests for record/playback and timing normalization
- Snapshot tests for recorder UI states
- Determinism regression tests
- PTY E2E scripts with verbose logs and captured event traces

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Record + replay produces identical UI output
- [ ] Scenarios run without drift
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Input Macro Recorder + Scenario Runner
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:09.809528710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:49:58.644231063Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:49:58.644213009Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed (bd-2lus.1-9 all closed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":374,"issue_id":"bd-2lus","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- FrankenTUI already has input macro utilities. A UI layer and scenario runner would be a standout demo.

Goals
- Record, edit, and replay input macros from the UI.
- Provide a guided demo mode that plays back scenarios reliably.
- Allow snapshot capture at key frames.

Testing
- Unit tests for recorder/playback logic and timing normalization.
- PTY E2E tests that record and replay with verbose event logs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lus.1","title":"Macro Recorder spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Controls: Record / Stop / Play / Loop / Speed
- Timeline view with markers (start/end, key events)
- Failure behavior: if playback fails, show error state + log
- Scenario runner: list of curated macros with descriptions

## Timing Model
- Normalize timing so playback is stable across machines
- Optional fixed‑step playback for deterministic tests

## Tests (Required)
- Unit tests for control state machine
- Snapshot tests for timeline states
- PTY E2E: record → stop → replay

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear control flow and error states
- [ ] Deterministic playback mode specified
- [ ] Test matrix included

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec defines macro record/playback flow, scope (global vs screen), and safety rules.
- Includes unit-test plan for serialization, replay determinism, and input timing normalization.
- Includes E2E test plan that records a macro and replays it with JSONL logs (event stream, timing deltas, success/failure).
- Logging schema specifies macro id, event count, replay drift, and outcome fields.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:10.107631276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:36:44.883647377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:36:44.883623783Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lus.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2lus","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3coz","title":"Audit all Widget implementations and categorize by migration complexity","description":"## Purpose

Before migrating, we need a complete inventory of all Widget/StatefulWidget implementations
and categorize them by how they use the buffer parameter.

## Categories

### Category A: Simple buffer-only widgets
These just write to the buffer and don't need any Frame features.
Migration: Change `buf: &mut Buffer` to `frame: &mut Frame`, then use `frame.buffer` internally.

### Category B: Widgets needing hit testing
These need mouse interaction (buttons, links, list items).
Migration: Use `frame.hit_grid` to register clickable regions.

### Category C: Widgets needing cursor control
These are input widgets that need to position the cursor.
Migration: Use `frame.cursor_position` and `frame.cursor_visible`.

### Category D: Widgets that could benefit from degradation
Complex widgets that could simplify rendering under time pressure.
Migration: Check `frame.degradation` and adapt rendering.

## Deliverable

A comment on this bead with:
1. List of all widgets by file
2. Category assignment for each
3. Estimated complexity (trivial/medium/complex)
4. Any special considerations

## Files to check

- crates/ftui-widgets/src/*.rs (all widget files)
- Look for `impl Widget for` and `impl StatefulWidget for`","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:21:44.614299089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:26:11.118240996Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:25:01.332183353Z","close_reason":"Widget audit complete. All 15+ widget files migrated to Frame API. debug_overlay.rs (feature-gated) also verified migrated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":424,"issue_id":"bd-3coz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Widget Migration Audit Report

### Summary
Almost all widgets have already been migrated to Frame! Only `debug_overlay.rs` remains.

### Migrated Widgets (Category A - already complete)
All widgets below already use `frame: &mut Frame`:

| File | Widget | Status |
|------|--------|--------|
| block.rs | Block | Migrated |
| input.rs | TextInput | Migrated (Category C - uses cursor) |
| list.rs | List, ListState | Migrated |
| paragraph.rs | Paragraph | Migrated |
| progress.rs | ProgressBar | Migrated |
| rule.rs | Rule | Migrated |
| scrollbar.rs | Scrollbar, ScrollbarState | Migrated |
| spinner.rs | Spinner, SpinnerState | Migrated |
| status_line.rs | StatusLine | Migrated |
| table.rs | Table, TableState | Migrated |
| padding.rs | Padding (Widget + StatefulWidget) | Migrated |
| panel.rs | Panel | Migrated |
| constraint_overlay.rs | ConstraintOverlay | Migrated |
| error_boundary.rs | FallbackWidget, ErrorBoundary | Migrated |
| cached.rs | CachedWidget (test widgets) | Migrated |

### Remaining Widgets (debug_overlay.rs - feature-gated)
| Widget | Line | Category | Complexity |
|--------|------|----------|------------|
| DebugOverlay | 226 | A (buffer-only) | Trivial |
| DebugOverlayStateful<W: StatefulWidget> | 355 | A | Trivial |
| DebugOverlayStateful<W: Widget> | 391 | A | Trivial |
| StubWidget (test) | 443 | A | Trivial |

All remaining widgets are Category A (simple buffer access only) - they just need buf changed to frame and internal buf. calls changed to frame.buffer.

### Migration Plan
1. Fix debug_overlay.rs (4 implementations)
2. Update tests to use Frame::new() instead of Buffer::new()

### Estimated Effort
Trivial - mechanical find/replace changes only. No Frame features needed (hit testing, cursor, degradation) in these widgets.
","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:23:39Z"},{"id":425,"issue_id":"bd-3coz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Widget Audit Results

### Summary
Found 16 widget implementations across 13 files in `crates/ftui-widgets/src/`.

### Category A: Simple buffer-only widgets (just write to buffer)
Migration: Change `buf: &mut Buffer` to `frame: &mut Frame`, use `frame.buffer` internally.

| Widget | File | Complexity | Notes |
|--------|------|------------|-------|
| Block | block.rs | **Trivial** | Just draws borders and fills background |
| Paragraph | paragraph.rs | **Trivial** | Renders multi-line text |
| Rule | rule.rs | **Trivial** | Horizontal divider line |
| StatusLine | status_line.rs | **Trivial** | Status bar with regions |
| FallbackWidget | error_boundary.rs | **Trivial** | Simple error display |
| DebugOverlay | debug_overlay.rs | **Medium** | Draws debug boundaries |

### Category B: Widgets needing hit testing (mouse interaction)
Migration: Use `frame.hit_grid` to register clickable regions.

| Widget | File | Complexity | Notes |
|--------|------|------------|-------|
| List | list.rs | **Medium** | Selection state; click-to-select |
| Table | table.rs | **Medium** | Row selection; click-to-select |
| Scrollbar | scrollbar.rs | **Medium** | Thumb dragging |

### Category C: Widgets needing cursor control (input widgets)
Migration: Use `frame.cursor_position` and `frame.cursor_visible`.

| Widget | File | Complexity | Notes |
|--------|------|------------|-------|
| TextInput | input.rs | **Complex** | cursor_position() method needed |

### Category D: Widgets benefiting from degradation
Many widgets already have degradation support:
- ProgressBar, Spinner, Scrollbar, Rule - already implemented

### Wrapper/Meta Widgets
| Widget | File | Complexity | Notes |
|--------|------|------------|-------|
| CachedWidget | cached.rs | **Medium** | Caching wrapper |

### Migration Priority
1. **High**: TextInput (cursor API), List (hit testing)
2. **Medium**: Table, CachedWidget, DebugOverlay
3. **Low**: Block, Paragraph, Rule, StatusLine (trivial)

### Key Finding
**Good news**: Widget trait already uses Frame! All widgets have signature:
`fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame)`

Migration is primarily about:
1. Adding hit_grid registrations for Category B
2. Adding cursor_position calls for Category C (TextInput)
3. Ensuring CachedWidget properly forwards Frame context
","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:26:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cuk","title":"Text Effects: Comprehensive Test Suite","description":"## Background
Text effects are highly visual and easy to break. This bead is the **canonical, aggregated QA gate** for the entire text‑effects system. It does NOT replace per‑effect tests in each feature bead; it **collects them into one coherent test plan** so nothing is forgotten and CI has a single high‑signal target.

## Scope
- Unit tests (core math + every effect type)
- Snapshot tests (visual regression)
- Benchmarks (performance budgets)
- E2E script (headless demo + timing logs)

---

## 1) Unit Tests (crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs #[cfg(test)])

```rust
mod tests {
    // === Easing (10 tests) ===
    test_easing_linear_identity
    test_easing_input_clamped
    test_easing_bounds_normal
    test_easing_elastic_overshoots
    test_easing_back_overshoots

## 4) E2E Script (scripts/demo_text_effects_e2e.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== Text Effects E2E Tests ==='
echo "Date: $(date)"

# Build release
echo 'Building...'
cargo build -p ftui-demo-showcase --release 2>&1 | tail -1

# Run demo in headless mode with timeout
# The demo writes render stats to stderr
echo 'Running visual effects screen...'
timeout 10s cargo run -p ftui-demo-showcase --release -- \
    --screen visual_effects \
    --headless \
    --ticks 100 \
    2>&1 | tee /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log

# Check for panics
if grep -q 'panic' /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log; then
    echo 'FAIL: Panic detected!'
    exit 1
fi

# Check render times
avg_ms=$(grep 'avg_render_ms' /tmp/text_effects_e2e.log | tail -1 | cut -d= -f2)
if (( $(echo "$avg_ms > 16" | bc -l) )); then
    echo "WARN: Render time ${avg_ms}ms exceeds 16ms budget"
fi

echo 'All E2E tests passed!'
```

 {"id":"bd-3cwi","title":"Performance Benchmark Validation Suite","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI has strict performance requirements as documented in AGENTS.md:
- 16-byte Cell for SIMD comparisons
- Row-major buffer layout for cache prefetching
- Sub-millisecond execution for typical operations
- 64KB buffered output (one write per frame)

This bead creates a comprehensive benchmark suite to validate these requirements.

## Benchmarks to Create

### Cell Operations (crates/ftui-render/benches/cell_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn cell_comparison_simd_aligned() { /* 4 cells per cache line */ }
#[bench] fn cell_default_construction() { /* <10ns */ }
#[bench] fn cell_clone_16_bytes() { /* verify no heap allocation */ }
```

### Buffer Operations (crates/ftui-render/benches/buffer_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn buffer_create_80x24() { /* <100μs */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_create_200x50() { /* <500μs */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_fill_80x24() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn buffer_row_major_scan() { /* verify cache efficiency */ }
```

### Diff Computation (crates/ftui-render/benches/diff_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn diff_identical_buffers() { /* O(1) fast path */ }
#[bench] fn diff_single_cell_change() { /* <10μs */ }
#[bench] fn diff_full_screen_change_80x24() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn diff_full_screen_change_200x50() { /* <5ms */ }
```

### Presenter (crates/ftui-render/benches/presenter_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_no_change() { /* <1μs */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_single_cell() { /* <10μs */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_emit_full_screen() { /* <5ms */ }
#[bench] fn presenter_state_tracking() { /* verify no redundant SGR */ }
```

### Widget Rendering (crates/ftui-widgets/benches/widget_bench.rs)
```rust
#[bench] fn paragraph_render_100_chars() { /* <100μs */ }
#[bench] fn list_render_100_items() { /* <1ms */ }
#[bench] fn table_render_10x10() { /* <500μs */ }
```

## Performance Budget Enforcement

Create `scripts/bench_budget.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BUDGETS=(
    'cell_comparison:100ns'
    'buffer_create_80x24:100μs'
    'diff_full_screen:1ms'
    'presenter_emit_full:5ms'
)

cargo bench --bench '*' -- --save-baseline current 2>/dev/null

for budget in "${BUDGETS[@]}"; do
    name=${budget%%:*}
    limit=${budget##*:}
    actual=$(cargo bench --bench '*' -- $name --noplot 2>/dev/null | grep 'time:' | awk '{print $3}')
    echo "[$name] Budget: $limit, Actual: $actual"
done
```

## CI Integration

Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
```yaml
benchmark:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
    - run: cargo bench --bench '*' -- --noplot
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: benchmark-results
        path: target/criterion
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Cell benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Buffer benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Diff benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Presenter benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] Widget benchmarks created and passing
- [ ] bench_budget.sh script working
- [ ] CI job configured (warn-only, not blocking)
- [ ] All benchmarks have documented budgets
- [ ] No benchmark exceeds 2x budget (panic threshold)

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.7","title":"Determinism: record/replay web sessions (input + resize + ticks)","description":"Goal
- Ensure we can deterministically reproduce bugs and perf issues in the browser.

Requirements
- Record: input events, resize events, tick deltas, and any nondeterminism sources.
- Replay: drive ftui-web runner to exact same rendered output checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A recorded session replays to identical frame checksums on same build.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","title":"Optimize: wasm binary size + startup time (LTO, feature gating)","description":"Goal
- Make the web demo load fast and keep wasm artifacts small.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured size targets with before/after numbers.
- Startup (time-to-first-frame) improved with profiling evidence.
","notes":"Progress 2026-02-09: Added wasm-pack release profile optimization in crates/frankenterm-web/Cargo.toml: wasm-opt=["-Oz","--all-features"]. Revalidated with controlled comparison: baseline wasm-pack --release --no-opt size=237282 bytes; optimized size=214025 bytes (delta -23257, -9.80%). Startup proxy via Node WebAssembly.compile (n=150): avg 0.5538ms -> 0.5416ms (2.2% better), p95 0.7960ms -> 0.6986ms (12.2% better). Quality gates green in isolated target dir: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo check/test -p frankenterm-web (native + wasm target).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:20:47.970869049Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:20:47.970851677Z","close_reason":"Completed optimization slice: wasm-pack release wasm-opt [-Oz,--all-features], measured size 237282 -> 214025 bytes (-9.80%), startup proxy p95 improved 0.7960ms -> 0.6986ms; quality gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
